---
source_file: Phd courses/Information theory for natural scientists/Quizzes/ITFNS Practice Quiz 3 - Questions.md
---

## Q1 maximal useful work
The maximal useful work extractable from a system held at reservoir temperature T is the drop in its

- [ ] energy E, since all of the internal energy converts to work
- [ ] entropy S, once multiplied by the reservoir temperature T
- [x] free energy F = E − TS, the available energy at fixed T

location: ITFNS Ch. 1

## Q2 additivity of Boltzmann entropy
Boltzmann's entropy S = ln Ω is additive over independent subsystems because

- [x] the state counts multiply, Ω = Ω₁Ω₂, and log of a product adds
- [ ] the energies of the two subsystems simply add up together
- [ ] the temperatures of the subsystems become equal at equilibrium

location: ITFNS Ch. 1

## Q3 negative absolute temperature
Negative absolute temperature can arise in a system whose

- [ ] parts have been cooled well below their own freezing point
- [x] energy spectrum is bounded above, allowing inverted populations
- [ ] kinetic energy is unbounded, so any high energy is available

location: ITFNS Ch. 1

## Q4 three bits
3 bits of information shrink the space of possibilities

- [ ] 6 times, adding the three binary choices up together
- [x] 8 times, each bit halving the space, so that 2³ = 8
- [ ] 9 times, taking the square of the three-bit count

location: ITFNS Ch. 2

## Q5 typical sequences
For a long sequence from an alphabet with entropy S per symbol, the number of typical sequences grows like

- [x] 2^{NS}, far fewer than the 2^N total for unequal symbols
- [ ] n^N, so that all sequences of that length count as typical
- [ ] 2^N, the same count whatever the symbol statistics are

location: ITFNS Ch. 2

## Q6 conditional entropy
The conditional entropy S(B|A) is equal to

- [ ] S(A) + S(B), the sum of the two separate entropies
- [ ] S(B) − S(A), the difference of the two separate entropies
- [x] S(A,B) − S(A), the joint entropy less that of the condition

location: ITFNS Ch. 2

## Q7 fully noisy channel
A binary symmetric channel that flips each bit with probability 1/2 has capacity

- [ ] 1 bit, since it still passes one bit on every single use
- [ ] 1/2 bit, since half of the bits still get through correctly
- [x] 0, since output and input are statistically independent

location: ITFNS Ch. 2

## Q8 demon's cost
For the demon, the energetic cost of a full measurement-and-erasure cycle is bounded below by

- [ ] the free-energy change of the working gas taken by itself
- [ ] zero, since the measure and erase steps cancel out exactly
- [x] T times the system–memory mutual information that is built up

location: ITFNS Ch. 3

## Q9 information is life
In the "information is life" picture, natural selection is best described as

- [ ] the survival of the strongest organisms within a population
- [ ] a random drift of gene frequencies that carries no information
- [x] an ever more efficient encoding of the environment in the genes

location: ITFNS Ch. 3

## Q10 Kelly gambling
In proportional ("Kelly") gambling, to maximize long-run growth you bet on each outcome

- [ ] all of it on the single most probable outcome each round
- [ ] an equal share on every outcome, so as to hedge one's risk
- [x] a share equal to that outcome's own true probability pᵢ

location: ITFNS Ch. 3

## Q11 thermodynamics predicts no numbers *(True/False)*
Thermodynamics does not predict absolute numerical values; it sets inequalities and relations among quantities that follow from conservation laws.

- [x] True
- [ ] False

location: ITFNS Ch. 1

## Q12 entropy at equilibrium *(True/False)*
For a closed system, the entropy reaches its minimum in thermal equilibrium.

- [ ] True
- [x] False

location: ITFNS Ch. 1

## Q13 entropy and certainty *(True/False)*
The entropy −Σ pᵢ log pᵢ is maximal when one outcome is certain.

- [ ] True
- [x] False

location: ITFNS Ch. 2

## Q14 sign of mutual information *(True/False)*
Mutual information I(A;B) can become negative when A and B happen to be anticorrelated.

- [ ] True
- [x] False

location: ITFNS Ch. 2

## Q15 channel capacity *(select one or more)*
Which statements about channel capacity are correct?

- [x] it is the mutual information maximized over the input statistics
- [x] it is the top rate of asymptotically error-free transmission
- [ ] it is simply the raw output entropy S(B) taken on its own

location: ITFNS Ch. 2
